evidence.description: EditorAction discriminated union is closed at 11 variants: NoteBodyEdited, BlurEvent, IdleTimerFired, DomainSnapshotReceived, NoteFileSaved, NoteSaveFailed, RetryClicked, DiscardClicked, CancelClicked, CopyClicked, NewNoteClicked. The §2 cross-product narrative explicitly states '11 actions × 5 statuses = 55 cells'. Past-tense names align with behavioral-spec.md §3.1 (NoteFileSaved, NoteSaveFailed).
CRIT-001 frontmatter description and passThreshold reference NoteFileSaved/NoteSaveFailed/NoteBodyEdited only — no SaveSuccess/SaveFailed orphans remain in CRIT-001.
CRIT-007 passThreshold isDirty transition table uses 'editing+NoteBodyEdited→true, saving+NoteFileSaved→false, saving+NoteSaveFailed→true retained' — fully aligned with the closed union.
CRIT-008 description and passThreshold reference NoteFileSaved consistently — no SaveSuccess orphan remains.
§2 editorReducer prose now states isDirty transitions and cancel-idle-timer command emission in terms of NoteBodyEdited/NoteFileSaved/NoteSaveFailed exclusively.
§5 Pass Criteria table CRIT-001 and CRIT-008 rows reference past-tense action names consistently.
CRIT-001/008 prose sections reference NoteBodyEdited/NoteFileSaved/NoteSaveFailed in line with the closed union.
§1 introduction PROP-EDIT range updated to 'PROP-EDIT-001..040, including 020a/020b' — consistent with new CRIT-012/PROP-EDIT-040 binding.

CRIT-007 (reducer totality) covers the full status × action cross-product (5 × 11 = 55 cells) with PROP-EDIT-007 fast-check assertion of state.status ∈ 5-variant enum and commands[].kind ∈ 9-variant EditorCommand union.
CRIT-006 covers canCopy edge cases over all 5 status values × arbitrary body strings via PROP-EDIT-006 ≥100 fast-check runs.
CRIT-009 names concrete boundary cases (at-threshold, one-ms-before, one-ms-after, lastSaveAt>fireAt) for computeNextFireAt locked signature.
CRIT-010 enumerates all 9 EditorCommand variants and binds Set-membership assertion to PROP-EDIT-007 fast-check property; tsc --noEmit --strict --noUncheckedIndexedAccess exit 0 is required.
CRIT-009 locks computeNextFireAt signature to {lastEditAt, lastSaveAt, debounceMs, nowMs}: {shouldFire, fireAt} per RD-019 with caller-supplied nowMs.
CRIT-011 binds classifySource to literal-union return type and Phase 5 canonical purity grep zero-hit requirement.

Weight arithmetic sums to 1.00: 0.09+0.11+0.10+0.10+0.10+0.10+0.10+0.08+0.07+0.07+0.05+0.03 = 1.00. Each CRIT has a distinct dimension binding and a binary-evaluable pass threshold naming concrete test files and property names.

CRIT-012 (DomainSnapshotReceived mirroring) is binary-evaluable, non-overlapping with CRIT-001 (which now scopes out snapshot mirroring per CRIT-001 prose lines 259), and binds to two concrete test file/property names.
§6 Forbidden in This Sprint enumerates the canonical purity-audit pattern verbatim from verification-architecture.md §2.

PROP-EDIT-040 is now declared in §4 Proof Obligations with property statement 'editorReducer(s, { kind: \'DomainSnapshotReceived\', snapshot: S }).state.{status, isDirty, currentNoteId, pendingNextNoteId} === S.{status, isDirty, currentNoteId, pendingNextNoteId} for any s and any S', Tier 2, Tool: fast-check + vitest, Required: true, Pure-or-Shell: Pure. Iter-2 FIND-002 remediated.
PROP-EDIT-040 also appears in the Tier 2 (§3 Verification Tier Assignments) prose with citation to FIND-002 remediation and behavioral-spec.md §3.4a / REQ-EDIT-014.

Coverage Matrix row for REQ-EDIT-014 now references PROP-EDIT-040: 'PROP-EDIT-029 (state ownership boundary), PROP-EDIT-040 (snapshot mirroring, Tier 2 fast-check)' with test paths editorReducer.test.ts and editorReducer.property.test.ts.

CRIT-012 frontmatter passThreshold names the deterministic unit test 'DomainSnapshotReceived mirrors S.status/isDirty/currentNoteId/pendingNextNoteId' in editorReducer.test.ts and the fast-check property 'snapshot mirroring is identity over state fields' in editorReducer.property.test.ts — concrete and binary-evaluable.

Pure-tier purity boundary is preserved by §2 forbidden-API list and §6 canonical purity grep pattern (verbatim from verification-architecture.md §2). All three pure modules (editorPredicates.ts, editorReducer.ts, debounceSchedule.ts) carry MUST NOT import @tauri-apps/api obligation.
